ab: karuwa ku muka kirepanauwa kanu nay?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: emdaamaw! salaw ku semangal!
ch: B:可以，我很高興。

ab: karuwa ku muka kiyumalra dra manay driyan kanu nay?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: aeman! adri sadru ku kinalradraman.
ch: B:不要，我所知道的不多。
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: piyawari yu an mukidrayayan na kadaw? maranger ku muka kirepanauwa kanu. wawai yu nay?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: aiwa! ti ngarayaw yu i ruma.
ch: B:好，我會在家等你。
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: piyawari yu an daman? maranger ku kiyumalr kanu dratu batibatiyan dra Pinuyumayan.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於卑南族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I'd like to ask you about the Peinan legend.

ab: auka ku i drenan an daman. uniyan ku i ruma.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: ku kinilrengawan i, malradram yu dratu kakuwayanan kana Pinuyumayan kema. maranger ku kipaladam kanu driya.
ch: A:我聽說您對卑南族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Peinan people and I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: adri sadru nanku kinalradraman. amawna, pakirungetra ku temuban.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: kiyumalra ku driya! karuwa ku semasing pasirep kanu ngai nay? likudran i, ta kalralreayan temililr emapetr.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: aiwa! ta kuwadruwaw i!
ch: B:好的，就這麼辦。
en: B: Okay, no problem (just do it).

ab: kiyumalra ku driya! munuma lra nu ami garem?
ch: A:請問您今年幾歲了?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: makaiwa misama dra waluwa ku ami lra.
ch: B:我已經98歲了。
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: lralrak an menau ta kanu.
ch: A:您看起來很年輕。
en: A: You look very young.

ab: a lralrak dra manay. maidrang ku lra.
ch: B:哪有年輕!我已經老了。
en: B: Not young! I'm already old.

ab: kiyumalra ku driya! kemay yu isuwa?
ch: A:請問您來自哪裡?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: kemay ku i Puyuma.
ch: B:我來自普悠瑪(南王部落)。
en: B: I'm from the Puyuma (Southern King tribe).

ab: kiyumalra ku driya! nu kataguwin i, amau a Pinuyumayan?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是卑南族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Puyuma?

ab: amelri! a Payrang taytaw.
ch: B:不，她是閩南人。
en: B: No, she is a Minnanese.

ab: amanamanay ngaiyan nu kinasaiguan marengay?
ch: A:您會說哪些語言?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: saigu ku temaraamis aw temarapuyuma.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I speak Aramaic and Puyuma.

ab: salaw ku semangal draku iyadruwa kirepanauwa kanu garem. babati driya dratu lregiyan dra paaalrup maw?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: aiwa! a lralrak ku driya i, marayas ku muka i drenan paalrupa.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: semangalan! nu pinaladaman garem i, salaw inaba.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: panaan nanku nirengayan. amau tu batibatiyan dra temuwamuwan.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: ta palruwanay kadrini ta pinadrayaran garem. semangalan dranu pinaladaman.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: adri kiyagela! madrayadrayara ta driya.
ch: B:不客氣，我們再聊。
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: uwaburek ku lra! an masalr lra i, kilrengawa ta driya dra sadru dra batibatiyan.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: aiwa! tawar lra maw!
ch: B:好喔!請慢走。
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
